title: Sasha ot Sulem's dialect
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Conjecture|legends}}

}}
{{Quote|I can understand Mandalorian, but your words are all mixed up.|[[Revan/Legends|Revan]], to [[Sasha ot Sulem]]|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
A young [[Gender/Legends|woman]] by the name of [[Sasha ot Sulem]] made use of a unique '''dialect''' to converse. As a [[Youngling/Legends|child]], Sasha ot Sulem had been kidnapped from her family by [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] raiders. After spending [[Standard year/Legends|years]] among them, Sulem was eventually able to flee their custody and stow away aboard the ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'', a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] belonging to the former [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]. When the reformed [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] found Sulem, he attempted to convince Sulem to return home to her family, but found she spoke in a dialect unique to her, though Revan thought the [[Language/Legends|language]] bore some resemblance to the Mandalorian language<ref name="KotOR" /> of [[Mando'a/Legends|''Mando'a'']].<ref name="TMPaC">{{InsiderCite|86|The Mandalorians: People and Culture}}</ref> Despite his unfamiliarity with the strange dialect, through practice and possibly [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], Revan managed to converse well enough with the young woman to persuade her to leave the ''Ebon Hawk'' with the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] [[Lur Arka Sulas]], who had been searching for Sasha ot Sulem on behalf of her [[Parent/Legends|father]], [[Rundil ot Sulem]].

==Words and phrases==
This language included the following sentences or words:
[[File:Sasha.jpg|thumb|right|225px|[[Sasha ot Sulem]]]]
*''abds'' - hurt
*''bristag'' - spaceship
*''esowon'' - to see
*''hoot bad liaz Manlorey?'' - Why are you scared of the Mandalorians?
*''gon-disen'' - to love
*''in''- in
*''kapsie''- speak/talk
*''kesta-won'' - take, move
*''laesfa'' - house, home
*''ma'' - I; me; my
*''ma na esowon'' - I'm hiding
*''ma voule kesta-won miam nu ghis'' - I'd like some food right now
*''manlorey'' - mandalorian
*''yum'' - food
*''na'' - [negation adverb]
*''na abds'' - don't hurt me!
*''na esowon'' - to hide
*''na nu ghis'' - not now; before
*''nu ghis'' - now
*''stin-quiw'' - sleep
*''tabed'' - talk about
*''tabed me'' - tell me
*''wooba'' - my
*''wooba soosa __'' - my name is __
*''yooba'' - you
*''yooba palkie'' - You have to go
*''Yb -'' Goodbye/bye

==Behind the scenes==
The dialect and its primary speaker, Sasha ot Sulem, first appeared in the ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic|''Knights of the Old Republic'']], created by [[BioWare]] and released [[July 15]], [[2003]]. Dialogue between Sasha ot Sulem and the player character, Revan, serves as a minor puzzle, wherein the player is required to decipher and respond to the unfamiliar language in order to further the questline and aid Sulem.

While speaking with Sulem, Revan states that he understands the Mandalorian language—suggesting some similarity between the two dialects—but notes that Sulem's dialect is different, her words "mixed up" and altered into a unique language removed from the one spoken by the Mandalorian people. The Mandalorian language proper was later developed as a functional [[:Wikipedia:Constructed language|constructed language]] by musical composer [[Jesse Harlin]] and [[Author/Legends|author]] [[Karen Traviss]] for the [[Star Wars: Republic Commando|''Republic Commando'' game]] and [[Republic Commando|novel series]]. The true Mandalorian language, given the name ''Mando'a'', bears little resemblance to the dialect spoken by Sasha ot Sulem, with a number of specific vocabulary deviations evident between Sulem's dialogue and the established Mandalorian lexicon.

*''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]''
